symptom: Furnace ignites briefly (4-7 seconds) then shuts down. Blower continues. No heat. Often presents as intermittent no-heat, worse after a humid day.
diagnosis: Dirty or failing flame sensor. Oxide buildup prevents the board from detecting flame, triggering flame-loss shutdown.
procedure: Kill power at the furnace switch.
Locate the flame sensor — single metal rod with a ceramic insulator, mounted at the burner tray, opposite the ignitor.
Remove the 1/4" hex-head screw holding the sensor bracket. Pull the sensor out carefully.
Disconnect the single sensor wire from the spade terminal.
Clean the sensor rod with fine steel wool or 400-grit emery cloth — light pressure, clean until dull metal is uniform. Do NOT sand the ceramic.
Wipe with a dry cloth. Reinstall in the same position.
Restore power and run a heat cycle. Flame should establish and stay lit. Blower should run after the fan-on delay.
If cleaning doesn't resolve the issue in the next cycle, replace with Lennox 10K5601.
safety_notes: Power must be OFF — control board can energize the ignitor unexpectedly during diagnostics.
Gas supply should be off at the appliance shutoff before removing the burner cover.
If you smell gas at any point, stop, ventilate, and flag_safety.
